name: golangci-lint Setup with Custom Rules
description: Install and configure golangci-lint with a curated set of linters and custom rules for consistent code quality.
trendingReason: golangci-lint is the industry standard for Go static analysis

steps.title: Install golangci-lint
steps.description: Install the latest version of golangci-lint using the official install script.
steps.tip: On macOS you can also use: brew install golangci-lint

steps.title: Run with default settings
steps.description: Run golangci-lint against your project to see baseline issues.

steps.title: Create a configuration file
steps.description: Write a .golangci.yml with curated linters and settings for your project.
steps.command:
cat > .golangci.yml << 'EOF'
run:
  timeout: 5m

linters:
  enable:
    - errcheck
    - govet
    - staticcheck
    - unused
    - gosimple
    - ineffassign
    - misspell
    - gofmt
    - revive
    - gocritic

linters-settings:
  revive:
    rules:
      - name: exported
        severity: warning
  gocritic:
    enabled-tags:
      - diagnostic
      - style

issues:
  exclude-rules:
    - path: _test\.go
      linters:
        - errcheck
EOF

steps.title: Run with the custom configuration
steps.description: Execute the linter using your new config file.

steps.title: Fix auto-fixable issues
steps.description: Apply automatic fixes for issues that golangci-lint can resolve.
steps.tip: Review auto-fixed changes before committing. Not all fixes are perfect.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# golangci-lint Setup and Run
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Checking golangci-lint ==="
if ! command -v golangci-lint &> /dev/null; then
  echo "Installing golangci-lint..."
  curl -sSfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/golangci/golangci-lint/master/install.sh | sh -s -- -b "$(go env GOPATH)/bin"
fi
golangci-lint --version

echo ""
echo "=== Running Linter ==="
golangci-lint run ./... --out-format colored-line-number || true

echo ""
echo "=== Lint Commands ==="
echo "Run:       golangci-lint run ./..."
echo "Fix:       golangci-lint run --fix ./..."
echo "Verbose:   golangci-lint run -v ./..."
echo "Specific:  golangci-lint run --enable gocritic ./..."
echo "Cache:     golangci-lint cache clean"

faq.question: Which linters should I enable for a new project?
faq.answer: Start with errcheck, govet, staticcheck, unused, gosimple, and revive. Add gocritic and misspell once your codebase is clean. Enable stricter linters incrementally.

faq.question: How do I suppress a specific lint warning?
faq.answer: Add a //nolint:lintername comment on the line. For entire files, use //nolint at the package line. Prefer fixing the issue over suppressing it.

faq.question: How do I integrate golangci-lint into CI?
faq.answer: Use the official GitHub Action: golangci/golangci-lint-action. It caches results between runs and supports config files automatically.
